dry_bulk.confluence_note: Quantitative signals (Z-scores, ROC, percentiles) align strongly with qualitative sentiment and momentum, despite persistent negative fundamentals.
dry_bulk.summary: The dry bulk market is in an EXPANSION regime with extreme Z-scores (BDI +2.81σ, Capesize +2.60σ) and 60-day ROCs exceeding +30% across all segments. Recent Breakwave reports highlight neutral-to-positive momentum, though fundamentals (e.g., China steel production down -4.5% YOY, iron ore inventories at multi-year highs) remain weak.
key_signals: China iron ore inventories +21.9% YOY but imports +3.4% YOY
Breakwave sentiment neutral but momentum positive in recent reports
dry_bulk.outlook: Near-term strength likely to persist, but macro risks (China slowdown, high inventories) could trigger volatility.
dry_bulk.watch: Monitor China steel demand and iron ore inventory drawdowns for signs of fundamental improvement.

tanker.confluence_note: Quantitative metrics (Z-scores, ROC, percentiles) strongly support qualitative sentiment and fundamentals, indicating a structural shift in the market.
tanker.summary: Tanker markets are in EXPANSION with Clean Tanker Z-scores at +1.95σ and Dirty Tanker ROC60 at +49.0%. Breakwave reports show consistent positive sentiment, momentum, and fundamentals since February 2026, driven by geopolitical risks (Middle East tensions) and inventory draws. VLCC rates remain elevated despite softening in May.
key_signals: Clean Tanker at 97.7th percentile over 5 years
Global crude oil on water +10.1% YOY (March)
tanker.outlook: Structural tightness in tanker markets likely to sustain high rates, though near-term softening may occur as panic bids fade.
tanker.watch: Track OPEC+ supply adjustments and Middle East geopolitical developments for rate catalysts.

macro_note: The confluence of extreme quantitative metrics and improving qualitative signals suggests a rare alignment of cyclical and structural factors across both dry bulk and tanker markets, though dry bulk remains vulnerable to China demand risks.
